question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the RepeatSpec with jitter(0.1) in PollingTopicListener by opening many subscriptions simultaneously, causing all pollers to synchronize their poll intervals (jitter collapse under load), creating periodic thundering-herd DB query bursts that spike DB CPU and cause query timeouts across all nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that SharedPollingTopicListener.getSharedListener() always returns the same topicMessages Flux regardless of the filter, meaning a single attacker subscription that causes an error in the shared Flux will terminate all subscriptions across all topics on that node simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the onBackpressureBuffer(listenerProperties.getMaxBufferSize(), BufferOverflowStrategy.ERROR) in SharedTopicListener.listen() by subscribing and then pausing consumption for longer than the buffer fill time, causing ERROR to be emitted which terminates the subscriber's stream and, if the shared Flux has no other subscribers, also cancels the upstream shared poll?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, interval).maxBackoff(interval.multipliedBy(4L)) in RedisTopicListener.subscribe() by triggering repeated errors in the Redis subscription, causing the retry scheduler to accumulate pending retry tasks that consume memory and scheduler threads, eventually degrading the node?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the cache() call on the Redis container Mono in RedisTopicListener, where a single failed connection attempt that gets cached causes all subsequent listen() calls to immediately receive the cached error, permanently disabling the Redis listener for all users on that node until restart?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that PollingTopicListener.poll() computes pageSize as Math.min(limit, listenerProperties.getMaxPageSize()) where limit can be Integer.MAX_VALUE when filter.hasLimit() is false, causing a DB query with an extremely large LIMIT clause that causes the DB query planner to choose a full table scan, degrading all nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the AtomicLong count in PollingTopicListener.PollingContext by subscribing with a very large limit value, causing the count to increment rapidly and the limit calculation (filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get()) to underflow to a negative value, resulting in unexpected behavior in the pageSize computation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the delaySubscription(interval, scheduler) in PollingTopicListener.listen() by opening many subscriptions simultaneously, causing all delayed subscriptions to fire at the same time after the interval, creating a synchronized burst of DB queries that overwhelms the connection pool and causes timeouts for all nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Flux.defer(() -> poll(context)) pattern in PollingTopicListener by subscribing and unsubscribing rapidly, causing the deferred poll to be re-evaluated on each subscription without proper cleanup, accumulating open DB result streams that exhaust DB cursor limits?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that TopicMessageFilter.subscriberId is randomly generated (RandomStringUtils.random(8)) and not validated server-side, allowing an attacker to submit filters with crafted subscriberId values that cause log injection or log flooding in the doOnSubscribe log.info() call, degrading node I/O performance?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the doOnSubscribe(s -> log.info(..., filter)) in PollingTopicListener by crafting a TopicMessageFilter with an extremely long or specially formatted toString() output, causing excessive log I/O that saturates disk write throughput and degrades node performance?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the publishOn(Schedulers.boundedElastic(), false, listenerProperties.getPrefetch()) in SharedTopicListener.listen() by opening many subscriptions with a large prefetch value, causing the scheduler to pre-fetch and buffer large amounts of data per subscription, exhausting heap memory across the node?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the share() operator in RedisTopicListener.subscribe() by being the last subscriber to cancel, triggering unsubscribe() which removes the topic from topicMessages, and then immediately re-subscribing to force a new Redis channel subscription, cycling this to exhaust Redis server connection limits?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Flux.fromStream(topicMessageRepository.findByFilter(newFilter)) in PollingTopicListener.poll() by subscribing with startTime=0, causing the stream to open a DB cursor over the entire topic message history, and then cancelling the subscription without consuming all results, leaving open DB cursors that exhaust the connection pool?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of a maximum subscription duration in listen() to maintain open gRPC streams indefinitely with no endTime, causing the node to accumulate open HTTP/2 streams until the gRPC server's max concurrent streams limit is reached, denying service to all new subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that CompositeTopicListener.getTopicListener() throws UnsupportedOperationException for unknown listener types, and if the listenerProperties.getType() can be influenced through any configuration endpoint, cause all listen() calls to throw uncaught exceptions that crash the reactive pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Micrometer tap(Micrometer.observation(observationRegistry)) in PollingTopicListener.listen() by opening many subscriptions, causing the ObservationRegistry to accumulate unbounded observation contexts that are never closed (due to subscription cancellation), leading to memory leak and eventual node OOM?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that SharedPollingTopicListener uses a single shared PollingContext with a single lastConsensusTimestamp AtomicLong, by subscribing and causing the poll to advance the timestamp, then cancelling, causing subsequent legitimate subscribers to miss messages that occurred before the advanced timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Flux.fromIterable(topicMessageRepository.findLatest(...)) in SharedPollingTopicListener.poll() by subscribing when the DB contains a very large number of recent messages, causing the iterable to load all results into memory at once, exhausting heap and crashing the node?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, interval).maxBackoff(interval.multipliedBy(4L))) in SharedPollingTopicListener by triggering repeated DB errors through subscription patterns, causing the retry scheduler to queue Long.MAX_VALUE retry attempts that consume scheduler memory and degrade the node?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the METRIC constant 'hiero_mirror_grpc_listener' is used as a metric name with a 'mode' tag in all listener implementations, by opening subscriptions across all three listener modes simultaneously, causing metric tag cardinality explosion in the MeterRegistry that degrades monitoring infrastructure shared across nodes?
